metadata.title: Peak Equity Optimizer — Real Estate Deal Analysis & ARV Platform
metadata.description: Real estate deal analysis for sellers and investors — verified ARV, live comps, MAO calculator, and free estimator. No credit card required.

home.heroTitle: You have one shot at this deal. Use a number you can defend.
home.heroSubtitle: Sellers: know your true net proceeds before you list. Investors: verify your ARV from live comps before you offer. PEO replaces guesswork with defensible, data-backed analysis - so you walk into every negotiation with confidence.
home.step1Title: Enter the address and deal terms
home.step1Desc: Share the property address and your key assumptions — purchase price, ARV, and repairs.
home.step2Desc: We pull live comps, property facts, and market data automatically — then run the numbers through a multi-gate confidence model.
home.step3Desc: Walk into every negotiation with a defensible number: verified ARV, MAO, profit, ROI, and a full risk score.
home.roleSellerDesc: Know your true net proceeds before you list. Comp-backed pricing range, timeline risk, negotiation pressure points, and a clear readiness plan — all before you sign anything.
home.roleInvestorDesc: Run the full analysis in minutes: verified ARV, MAO, profit, ROI, and stress scenarios. Stop guessing at offers — start building them from verified data.

home.stat2Desc: County assessor records and MLS comps pulled at the moment of analysis.
home.stat3Desc: Flip, BRRRR, rental, and wholesale — each with its own failure-point framework.

home.academyTeaserDesc: Free access to our methodology, formula stack, strategy guides, and downloadable templates. Understand the process before you pay for the platform.
home.pathsHeading: Built for both sides of the deal
home.problemTitle: Most real estate decisions run on unverified data
home.problemBody: You're pricing a home or building an offer using comps that haven't been qualified, an ARV that hasn't been stress-tested, and net proceeds calculated on the back of a napkin. When the appraisal comes in or the inspection surfaces, the unverified number is the first thing that breaks.
home.solutionTitle: PEO replaces guesswork with data you can show to a room
home.solutionBody: Live comps pulled at the moment of analysis. Verified ARV from actual closed sales — not an AVM. Net proceeds calculated to the line item. Every output includes a confidence score that tells you exactly how much to trust it.
home.heroTitleLine2: is all it takes to lose.
home.heroProblemLine: Most listings are priced on guesswork. Most offers are built on unverified ARVs. PEO fixes both — with live comps, verified data, and analysis you can show to a room.
home.credBadge1Label: Median of actual closed sales — not an AVM
home.credBadge2Label: County records + MLS comps at analysis time
home.credBadge3Label: 4 exit strategies, each with failure-point analysis
home.credBadge4Label: Free estimator — no signup, no credit card
home.roleInvestorAdvancedTitle: Full Platform — Comp Controls + Scenario Planning
home.roleInvestorAdvancedDesc: Fine-tune comp selection, run line-item rehab budgets, model stress scenarios, DSCR, and refi exit. Built for operators who underwrite at depth.
home.roleVendorDesc: List your services where the analysis happens. Verified vendor profiles surface when investors run deals that match your service category.
home.trustTeaserHeading: Every number is traceable. Every output is documented.
home.trustTeaserDesc: PEO does not produce estimates you take on faith. The methodology is open, the formulas are published, and every confidence score tells you exactly what it is based on.
home.trustCard1Body: The median sale price of qualified sold comparables — not an AVM. Actual closed transactions within a defined radius and recency window.
home.trustCard2Body: Each analysis passes through data quality, comp coverage, valuation consistency, and model completeness gates before a confidence tier is assigned.
home.trustCard3Body: PEO is not a licensed appraisal. Rural markets and off-market properties may have insufficient comp coverage. All limitations are disclosed at the output level.
home.dashboardHeading: See what the analysis looks like before you run it.
home.dashboardDesc: Both paths — seller net sheet and investor ARV — produce structured outputs you can act on immediately.
home.splitSellerHeading: Know your net before your agent names the price.
home.splitSellerPoint1: Comp-backed pricing range verified against actual closed sales in your market.
home.splitSellerPoint2: Line-item net proceeds — not just sale price minus commission.
home.splitSellerPoint3: Risk summary: what could reduce your number before closing.
home.splitInvestorHeading: Build offers from verified data, not gut instinct.
home.splitInvestorPoint1: Verified ARV from qualified sold comps — not an automated estimate.
home.splitInvestorPoint2: MAO, ROI, and profit for flip, BRRRR, rental, and wholesale strategies.
home.splitInvestorPoint3: Stress scenarios and confidence scoring on every deal.
home.vendorHeading: The directory built inside the deal workflow.
home.vendorDesc: Contractors, lenders, inspectors, and property managers — listed where investors and sellers are already working.
home.vendor1Body: Every vendor in the PEO marketplace has a reviewed profile. No anonymous listings, no unverified claims.
home.vendor2Body: Vendors surface when investors run analyses that match their service category — not in a separate directory they have to remember to visit.
home.vendor3Body: Profile analytics, lead tracking, and review responses — all available in the Vendor dashboard.
home.faqTeaser1A: PEO is a real-estate intelligence platform that helps sellers understand their net proceeds and helps investors analyze deals with verified ARV, live comps, and strategy-specific underwriting.
home.faqTeaser2Q: Where does the data come from?
home.faqTeaser2A: Paid tiers pull live county assessor records and MLS-recorded sold comparables at the time of analysis. Free tiers are manual-input only.
home.faqTeaser3Q: What is the difference between Investor Basic and Investor Advanced?
home.faqTeaser3A: Basic includes automatic ARV, comps, MAO, profit, ROI, and a summary report. Advanced adds comp controls, line-item rehab budgeting, scenario planning, DSCR, refi modeling, and advanced appendices.
home.faqTeaser4Q: Is the Seller tier a subscription?
home.faqTeaser4A: No. The Seller tier is a one-time payment per property analysis ($49). You pay when you run an analysis — not on a recurring basis.

general.title: General
general.a1: PEO is a real-estate intelligence platform that helps sellers understand their net proceeds and helps investors analyze deals with verified ARV, live comps, and strategy-specific underwriting — backed by transparent methodology, not guesswork.
general.a2: PEO serves two audiences: homeowners who want to understand their net proceeds before listing, and real estate investors who need rigorous deal analysis before making offers. The free estimator works for both. Paid tiers go deeper for each role.
general.q3: Is PEO affiliated with a brokerage or lender?
general.a3: No. PEO is an independent analysis platform. It produces data and analysis — it does not represent buyers, sellers, or lenders.

data.title: Data
data.q1: Where does the data come from?
data.a1: Paid tiers pull live county assessor records and MLS-recorded sold comparables at the time of analysis. Free tiers are manual-input only — no live data is accessed.
data.q2: What is VERIFIED ARV and how is it different from an estimate?
data.a2: VERIFIED ARV is the median sale price of qualified sold comparables within a defined radius and time window. It is based on actual closed transactions — not automated estimates, Zestimates, or tax assessments.
data.a3: Comparable sales data reflects recent transactions from our data providers. Paid tiers include data pulled at the time of analysis — not cached values from weeks ago.

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.a1: Yes. We follow strict data security practices including encrypted connections and access controls. Full details are in our Privacy Policy.
privacy.q2: Do you sell my data to third parties?
privacy.a2: No. Property addresses and deal inputs you enter are used only to produce your analysis. We do not sell, share, or broker user data.

pricingFaq.title: Pricing
pricingFaq.a1: Yes. You can cancel your subscription at any time from your account settings. You will retain access through the end of your current billing period.
pricingFaq.q2: What is the difference between Investor Basic and Investor Advanced?
pricingFaq.a2: Basic includes automatic ARV, comps, MAO, profit, ROI, and a summary report. Advanced adds comp controls, line-item rehab budgeting, scenario planning, DSCR, refi modeling, and advanced appendices.
pricingFaq.q3: Is the Seller tier a subscription?
pricingFaq.a3: No. The Seller tier is a one-time payment per property analysis ($49). You pay when you run an analysis — not on a recurring basis.

trust.title: Trust Center
trust.methodologyText: PEO combines live property facts, qualified sold comparables, and conservative underwriting formulas to produce a layered confidence score. The analysis runs through a multi-gate triage process — each gate evaluates data quality, comp coverage, valuation consistency, and model completeness. A deal that passes all gates receives a HIGH confidence tier. One that trips data or comp gates receives conditional status with visible flags.
trust.dataSourcesText: Primary sources include county assessor records pulled live at the time of analysis. Secondary sources include MLS-recorded sold transactions within a defined radius and recency window. Where primary and secondary data are unavailable, estimated fields are used with explicit disclosure. The confidence score reflects how much of the analysis rests on estimated vs. verified inputs.
trust.definitionsText: VERIFIED ARV is the median sale price of qualified sold comparables — not an AVM, not a Zestimate. MARKET ARV is the median active listing price within a comparable radius, shown for reference only and never used as an underwriting input. MAO (Maximum Allowable Offer) is calculated as ARV minus repairs, carrying costs, selling costs, and a required profit floor — the highest price at which the deal remains viable.
trust.limitationsText: PEO is not a licensed appraisal and should not be used as a substitute for one. Data availability varies significantly by market — rural and off-market properties may have insufficient comp coverage to produce a verified ARV. Property condition, deferred maintenance, and local market shifts that occur after the analysis date are not captured. All outputs are analytical tools to support — not replace — professional judgment.

glossary.pageSubtitle: Every term used in PEO — defined clearly, with formulas where applicable.

arv.short: The expected market value of a property after all planned renovations are complete. PEO's Verified ARV is the median sale price of qualified sold comparables — not an automated estimate.
arv.formula: Verified ARV = Median(qualifying sold comp prices)

basis.short: The total amount of money you have deployed into a deal — purchase price plus all renovation costs. Keeping basis well below ARV is the fundamental goal of value-add investing.

brrrr.short: Buy, Rehab, Rent, Refinance, Repeat. A strategy where you buy and renovate a property, rent it out, then refinance based on the new ARV to pull your capital back out and repeat the cycle.
brrrr.formula: Cash Left In = (Purchase + Repairs) − Refi Proceeds

capRate.short: Annual net operating income divided by the property's current value. Used to assess rental yield independent of financing. Higher cap rate = higher yield, but often higher risk.

carryingCosts.short: All ongoing costs during the hold period: interest, property taxes, insurance, utilities, and maintenance. These accumulate daily and erode profit on longer holds.
carryingCosts.formula: Monthly Carry ≈ Loan × Rate/12 + Tax/12 + Insurance/12

cashOnCash.short: Annual cash flow divided by the total cash you actually invested (down payment, closing costs, repairs). Measures the real return on your out-of-pocket capital.
cashOnCash.formula: CoC = Annual Cash Flow ÷ Cash Invested

compQuality.short: A 0–100 score assigned to each comparable sale based on how closely it matches the subject property. Weighted across geography (30%), recency (25%), size (20%), physical similarity (15%), and condition (10%).
compQuality.formula: Score = Geo(30) + Recency(25) + GLA(20) + Physical(15) + Condition(10)

concessions.short: Credits or repairs agreed to during negotiation — typically the seller pays buyer's closing costs or reduces price to cover identified issues. Deducted from net proceeds.

confidenceTier.short: PEO's four-level classification of how reliable an analysis is: HIGH (≥80), MEDIUM (60–79), LOW (40–59), VERY LOW (<40). Based on data quality, comp coverage, valuation consistency, and model completeness.

dealGrade.short: A letter grade summarizing overall deal quality. A = strong buy with high confidence; B = solid deal; C = proceed with caution; D = does not meet minimum return thresholds.

dealScore.short: A numeric score combining profit margin (30%), ROI (25%), confidence tier (25%), and stress-case performance (20%). Kill switch penalties and MAO cushion adjustments apply.
dealScore.formula: Score = Profit(30) + ROI(25) + Confidence(25) + Stress(20) ± adjustments

dispositionCosts.short: The total cost of selling the property — agent commissions, transfer taxes, title fees, and closing credits. Typically 7–10% of the sale price.
dispositionCosts.formula: Disposition Costs = Sale Price × Disposition Rate (default 9%)

dscr.short: Net operating income divided by annual debt service. Lenders require ≥1.25 for most loans. Below 1.0 means the property does not generate enough income to cover its mortgage.

emd.short: A good-faith deposit submitted with a purchase offer. Typically 1–2% of the offer price. Shows the seller you are a serious buyer. At risk if you default without a valid contingency.

equityCapture.short: The difference between the ARV and your total cost basis at the moment of purchase/completion. Represents instant equity created by buying below market and adding value.

gla.short: The total finished, above-grade living space in square feet. Used as a primary matching criterion when selecting comparable sales — comps within ±20% GLA of the subject property score higher.

holdTime.short: The number of months from purchase to sale (for flips) or from purchase to stabilization (for rentals and BRRRR). Every additional month adds carrying costs and reduces ROI.

killSwitch.short: A property condition flag that adds estimated repair costs to the analysis before the MAO is calculated. CRITICAL flags (foundation, septic) can downgrade a deal recommendation. Always check before offering.
killSwitch.formula: MAO adjustment = sum of midpoint costs for active kill switches

ltv.short: The loan amount as a percentage of the property's value. Most hard money lenders lend 65–75% LTV on ARV. Higher LTV = less cash required, but higher monthly payments.

mao.short: The highest price you can pay and still meet your required profit threshold. Calculated two ways — by the Protocol (ARV minus all costs and profit floor) and by the 70% Rule — and takes the lower of the two.
mao.formula: MAO = min(ARV − Repairs − Costs − Profit, ARV × 70% − Repairs)

marketArv.short: The median list price of active comparable listings in the area. Shown for reference only — PEO never uses Market ARV as an underwriting input because active listings are not yet sold.
marketArv.formula: Market ARV = Median(active comparable listing prices)

marketVelocity.short: How quickly homes are selling in the subject market, based on average days on market (DOM) and months of inventory (MOI). Fast market = seller advantage, shorter hold risk. Slow market = longer holds, exit compression risk.
marketVelocity.formula: MOI = Active Listings ÷ Monthly Sales Volume

noi.short: Gross rental income minus all operating expenses (vacancy, management, maintenance, taxes, insurance). Excludes debt service. The core measure of a rental property's earning power.

opexRate.short: The percentage of gross rent consumed by operating expenses — vacancy, property management (typically 8–10%), maintenance, insurance, taxes, and HOA. Industry standard ranges from 30–50%.
opexRate.formula: Operating Expenses = Monthly Rent × OpEx Rate

points.short: Upfront lender fees expressed as a percentage of the loan amount. 2 points = 2% of the loan, paid at closing. Hard money loans typically charge 1–3 points.
points.formula: Points Cost = Loan Amount × Points Rate

ppsf.short: Sale price divided by gross living area. Used to normalize comparisons between properties of different sizes. PEO uses PPSF to detect outlier comps via z-score analysis.

refiProceeds.short: The loan amount available after refinancing based on the property's new ARV. In a BRRRR, refi proceeds are used to pay back the acquisition loan and return capital for the next deal.

riskBand.short: A qualitative risk classification combining profit margin, ROI, confidence tier, and investor profile. Four bands: Low, Moderate, Elevated, High. Used alongside deal grade to summarize downside exposure.

roi.short: Estimated profit divided by total cash invested (for flips) or by total cost basis (for rentals). Annualized when the hold period is specified. Target: ≥15% for a balanced investor.

sharpeRatio.short: A risk-adjusted return metric: expected return divided by standard deviation of return scenarios. Higher Sharpe = better return per unit of risk. Excellent ≥1.5, Acceptable 1.0–1.5, Poor <1.0.
sharpeRatio.formula: Sharpe ≈ (Base ROI − Risk-Free Rate) ÷ Std Dev(Scenarios)

spread.short: The difference between your contract price and the end buyer's maximum offer. This is your gross profit in a wholesale deal. A spread below $5,000 is generally too thin to assign reliably.
spread.formula: Spread = End Buyer Max Offer − Your Contract Price

stressProfit.short: Projected profit after applying a conservative stress scenario: ARV reduced by 5% and repair costs increased by 15%. If stress profit is negative, the deal fails the viability check.

triage.short: PEO's multi-gate evaluation process that determines whether an analysis has sufficient data quality to produce a reliable result. Triage checks data, comps, valuation consistency, and model completeness before assigning a confidence tier.

verifiedArv.short: The median sale price of qualified sold comparable properties — not an automated valuation model (AVM), not a Zestimate. Based on actual closed transactions within a defined radius and time window.
verifiedArv.formula: Verified ARV = Median(qualified sold comp prices)

wholesaleAssignment.short: A contract right sold to an end buyer for a fee (the assignment fee). The wholesaler never takes title — they sell the right to purchase at the contracted price, profiting on the spread.
wholesaleAssignment.formula: Assignment Fee = Spread (End Buyer Max − Your Price)

sellers.metaDescription: Know your true net proceeds before you list. Comp-backed pricing range, timeline risk, negotiation pressure points, and a readiness plan — all in one $49 one-time analysis.
sellers.ogDescription: Stop guessing what your home will net. Get verified comps, pricing range, and risk analysis before you list, reduce price, or accept a buyer.
sellers.mythsHeading: Six myths that cost sellers money.
sellers.risksHeading: Eight risks that decide the outcome.
sellers.mitigationsHeading: Eight practices that protect your position.
sellers.heroTitle: Know your net before your agent names the price.
sellers.heroLead: Before you sign a listing agreement or accept an offer, you need one number: what you’ll actually walk away with. PEO shows you exactly that — comp-backed pricing range, verified net proceeds, and the risks that could cut into both.
sellers.heroBody: Selling real estate is not only about finding a buyer. It is about protecting value, understanding timing, and avoiding decisions that weaken your position before negotiations even begin.
sellers.panelFeature1: Disciplined pricing range and market-position context.
sellers.panelFeature2: Estimated net proceeds and seller-focused report outputs.
sellers.panelFeature3: Timeline context, pricing risk, and negotiation pressure points.
sellers.panelFeature4: Standalone software analysis — no brokerage or lender affiliation.
sellers.problemBody1: Many owners enter the selling process with incomplete pricing logic. They may rely on emotion, a single opinion, broad neighborhood averages, or a number that sounds encouraging but has not been pressure-tested against market position, proceeds, timing, and risk.
sellers.problemBody2: That uncertainty creates avoidable problems. A property can be priced too high and lose momentum, or priced too low and leave money on the table.
sellers.solutionBody1: Peak Equity Optimizer Seller Analysis turns the selling decision into a more disciplined process. Instead of focusing on one headline number alone, this analysis helps sellers evaluate the property through several practical lenses at once.
sellers.solutionBody2: The goal is not hype. The goal is clarity.
sellers.deliverablesTitle: Better pricing logic before the market forces the decision for you.
sellers.deliverablesSubtitle: A one-time analysis per property, designed to reduce uncertainty before you list, relist, reduce price, or negotiate with buyers.
sellers.deliverablePricingDesc: A more disciplined asking-price view structured to help you evaluate where the property may fit in the market and what that pricing decision may mean for interest, leverage, and execution.
sellers.deliverableProceedsDesc: Estimated net proceeds. Sale price is not the same as what you keep. This analysis helps frame the difference between gross price and likely proceeds after common transaction costs and concessions.
sellers.deliverableTimingDesc: Timeline and market-position context. Price is tied to timing. A property may be positioned to attract stronger attention quickly, or it may be entering the market in a way that creates drag.
sellers.deliverableOutputsDesc: Seller-focused report outputs. The package is designed to leave you with usable deliverables, not just abstract commentary.
sellers.includedBody: The seller package is designed to help you review the pricing case, understand your likely net outcome, and see the risks that can weaken execution before they become expensive.
sellers.includedItem1Desc: A structured summary of the pricing case, including the assumptions, rationale, and practical tradeoffs behind the suggested range.
sellers.includedItem2Desc: A plain-language proceeds view that helps translate sale assumptions into an expected financial outcome.
sellers.includedItem3Desc: Organized market support intended to help sellers understand pricing position more clearly before they go active or respond to buyers.
sellers.includedItem4Desc: A focused explanation of the major factors most likely to weaken price, extend timeline, or reduce net outcome.
sellers.outcomeBody: Sellers lose leverage when they start the process without a clear view of pricing, net proceeds, and risk. This analysis is built to help you understand those moving parts before the market, a buyer, or an inspection report starts changing the conversation.
sellers.outcomeBullet1: Review the likely tradeoff between price and timing.
sellers.outcomeBullet2: Understand what fees, credits, and concessions can do to the bottom line.
sellers.outcomeBullet3: Identify negotiation pressure points before they become surprises.
sellers.gainsTitle: Capital gains are one of the most misunderstood parts of selling.
sellers.gainsBody: Many owners assume tax is based only on the sale price, but that is not how the analysis works. In general, the tax question starts with gain, not simply proceeds, and gain depends heavily on the property’s adjusted basis.
sellers.gainsDd1: Under current IRS guidance, adjusted basis generally begins with what you paid for the property and may be increased by capital improvements or reduced by depreciation claimed.
sellers.gainsDd2: If the property qualifies as your main home and you meet the ownership and use tests, you may be able to exclude a significant portion of the gain from federal tax.
sellers.gainsDd3: Rental use, business use, prior depreciation, or combined-use history can change how gain is calculated and taxed. Depreciation recapture is a specific item that catches many sellers off guard.
sellers.gainsDd4: If Form 1099-S is issued, the sale generally must be reported even if all or part of the gain is excluded. Consult a qualified tax professional before closing.
sellers.gainsTakeawayTitle: Treat tax planning as a real sale-planning item.
sellers.gainsTakeawayBody: Before you make assumptions about what you will owe, keep records, understand your basis, review any major capital improvements, and treat tax implications as a real planning item rather than an afterthought.
sellers.gainsDisclaimer: Important note — We are not financial advisors, tax advisors, or attorneys. This content is educational in nature. Sellers should consult a qualified CPA or tax professional for advice specific to their property, holding period, use history, state, and entity structure.
sellers.whoForBody1: This seller analysis is designed for owners who want to make better decisions before they list, relist, reduce price, respond to buyer pressure, or consider alternative sale paths.
sellers.whoForBody2: It is especially useful for sellers who want a more disciplined pricing framework, better visibility into likely net proceeds, a clearer understanding of risk before going to market, more confidence during negotiation, and a more informed view of what the sale may actually mean financially.
sellers.ctaTitle: Get clarity on price, proceeds, and risk before you list.
sellers.ctaBody: Peak Equity Optimizer Seller Analysis is designed to help sellers understand pricing, net proceeds, and execution risk. It is an independent software product — not a brokerage service, not a lender, and not a guaranteed sale program.
sellers.ctaBullet1: Analysis is based on live comps and property facts at the time of review.
sellers.ctaBullet2: Results improve decision quality — they do not guarantee a specific sale price or timeline.
sellers.ctaBullet3: The purpose of the product is better information, not certainty.
sellers.myth1Body: "The highest list price protects me." Not necessarily. A price that is too aggressive can reduce early momentum, weaken buyer confidence, and create a slower, more reactive sale process.
sellers.myth2Body: "If I get a strong offer price, I win." Offer price alone is not the full outcome. Financing terms, contingencies, repair credits, appraisal exposure, and concession requests can materially change what the offer is really worth.
sellers.myth3Body: "Net proceeds are basically the sale price minus commission." Payoff amounts, taxes, credits, closing costs, deferred maintenance, and negotiation concessions can all affect what you actually keep.
sellers.myth4Body: "Minor problems do not matter if the house shows well." Presentation matters, but so do inspection findings, deferred maintenance, title matters, insurance complications, and buyer financing conditions.
sellers.myth5Body: "The market alone determines outcome." The market matters, but so do preparation, pricing discipline, negotiation control, property condition, documentation readiness, and timing.
sellers.myth6Body: "If it does not sell quickly, I should just keep cutting the price." Price reductions can be necessary, but repeated reactive cuts without a clear strategy can weaken positioning.
sellers.risk1Body: Weak assumptions create overpricing or underpricing. Overpricing reduces early traction. Underpricing creates unnecessary loss of value.
sellers.risk2Body: Gross price is not the final outcome. Credits, fees, payoff items, repairs, or concessions may do more to the final number than sellers expect.
sellers.risk3Body: Inspection findings can change leverage quickly. Deferred maintenance, systems issues, structural concerns, or environmental issues can lead to price reductions or deal failure.
sellers.risk4Body: Agreed price does not end the pressure. Even when a buyer agrees on price, the transaction may still face pressure if the appraisal comes in below contract value.
sellers.risk5Body: A financed buyer introduces approval uncertainty, timeline risk, appraisal sensitivity, and last-minute lender conditions.
sellers.risk6Body: Longer marketing periods increase carrying costs, stress, property fatigue, and pressure to negotiate from a weaker position.
sellers.risk7Body: A seller without a clear understanding of value and bottom-line economics is easier to pressure during inspection, appraisal, and contract revision stages.
sellers.risk8Body: Missing permits, incomplete records, title issues, insurance complications, and other legal or operational matters can disrupt execution.
sellers.mitigation1Body: Use a disciplined pricing process based on market support, property condition, realistic positioning, and likely buyer response.
sellers.mitigation2Title: Know your bottom line before you negotiate.
sellers.mitigation2Body: Understand estimated net proceeds, major cost exposures, and your minimum acceptable outcome before offers arrive.
sellers.mitigation3Body: It is better to identify major issues early than to be surprised during inspection when leverage has shifted to the buyer.
sellers.mitigation4Body: Gather invoices, improvement records, permits, warranties, lease information if applicable, tax records, and other material property documentation before going active.
sellers.mitigation5Body: Assume the buyer will look for reasons to renegotiate. Enter the process knowing which issues are small, which are material, and which should change your response.
sellers.mitigation6Title: Compare offers by quality, not just headline number.
sellers.mitigation6Body: Evaluate financing strength, contingencies, proof of funds, timeline, earnest money, and overall execution credibility.
sellers.mitigation7Title: Think in terms of total outcome.
sellers.mitigation7Body: A slightly lower but cleaner offer can be stronger than a higher offer with fragile financing, broad contingencies, or heavy concession exposure.
sellers.mitigation8Title: Treat tax planning as part of the sale decision.
sellers.mitigation8Body: Do not wait until after closing to think about gain, basis, records, depreciation history, or reporting obligations.

investors.metaDescription: Stop guessing on offers. Verified ARV, MAO, stress scenarios, and deal scoring for flip, BRRRR, rental, and wholesale — built for serious investors. Free to start.
investors.ogTitle: Institutional-Grade Deal Analysis — Peak Equity Optimizer
investors.ogDescription: Verified ARV from live comps, MAO calculation, strategy-specific metrics, and risk scoring. Free estimator — $99/mo for the full platform.
investors.heroTitle: Stop guessing at offers. Every bid you make needs a verified ARV behind it.
investors.heroLead: PEO gives investors verified ARV from actual closed comps, MAO calculated to the cent, and stress scenarios that show you what happens when the deal breaks bad. Built to protect profit — not just estimate it.
investors.heroBannerRest: explains the methodology, formulas, strategies, and templates behind the platform so you can understand the workflow before you scale into deeper underwriting.
investors.panelRow1Value: Comprehensive deal screening with strategy-specific metrics and risk scoring
investors.panelRow2Value: Flip, BRRRR, rental, and wholesale workflows under one framework
investors.panelRow4Value: Generate reports and share analysis with partners and lenders
investors.tiersTitle: Clear plan differences that move serious users upward.
investors.tiersSubtitle: Free access proves the workflow. Paid tiers include the full underwriting system, templates, and collaboration features that support real deal volume.
investors.tierFreeFeature1: Basic Quick Offer analysis for first-look qualification.
investors.tierFreeFeature2: Limited strategy exposure to prove fit before expansion.
investors.tierFreeFeature3: Built for fast screening, not final underwriting.
investors.tierBasicFeature1: Live data pulled the moment you enter an address.
investors.tierBasicFeature2: Median comp-backed ARV, MAO, profit, ROI, and summary report.
investors.tierBasicFeature3: Designed for active individual investors who need repeatable analysis.
investors.tierAdvancedFeature1: All Basic features plus comp controls and fine-tuning.
investors.tierAdvancedFeature2: Line-item rehab budgeting, scenario planner, DSCR, and refi modeling.
investors.tierAdvancedFeature3: Advanced appendices and exports for lenders and partners.
investors.platformTitle: What paid tiers include that the free tier does not.
investors.platformBody: The expansion path is built around depth, repeatability, and execution support rather than superficial feature padding.
investors.platformPanel1Desc: Strategy-specific metrics for flip, BRRRR, rental, and wholesale deals in one platform.
investors.platformPanel2Desc: Each strategy routes to the failure points and benchmarks that actually matter for that approach.
investors.platformPanel3Desc: Deal score, stress scenarios, and execution-ready templates that move analysis into action.
investors.platformPanel4Desc: Documentation designed for internal review, lender conversations, and partner presentations.
investors.workflowTitle: Build your offer from verified data, not guesswork.
investors.workflowBody: Peak Equity Optimizer is a standalone analysis platform. It produces verified ARV, MAO, stress scenarios, and strategy-specific outputs to support your own underwriting process, lender conversations, and partner presentations.
investors.workflowBullet1: Use the software to standardize your analysis before you offer.
investors.workflowBullet2: Export reports for lenders, partners, and your own records.
investors.workflowBullet3: No affiliation with any lender, broker, or capital provider.

howItWorks.metaTitle: How It Works — Peak Equity Optimizer
howItWorks.metaDescription: Three steps from raw data to a confident real estate decision. Enter the address, get verified ARV and live comps, then act on numbers you can actually defend.
howItWorks.ogTitle: How It Works — Peak Equity Optimizer
howItWorks.ogDescription: Enter an address. Get verified ARV, MAO, and risk scoring. Make offers and listing decisions backed by live data — not guesswork.
howItWorks.heroLead: Three steps from raw data to a clear, confident decision. No guesswork — just structured analysis built on verified inputs.
howItWorks.splitTitle: Sellers and investors use the same engine — with outputs tailored to each role.
howItWorks.ctaTitle: Try the free estimator or see full pricing.

academy.metaDescription: A comprehensive guide to real estate investing, built to help investors understand how opportunities are found, evaluated, and executed with discipline.
academy.ogDescription: A comprehensive guide to real estate investing, built to help investors understand how opportunities are found, evaluated, and executed with discipline.
academy.formulaStackMetaTitle: Formula Stack — Academy — Peak Equity Optimizer
academy.formulaStackMetaDescription: The canonical formulas behind every PEO analysis: Fix & Flip, BRRRR, Rental, and Wholesale. The same math the software runs.
academy.strategiesMetaDescription: Different deals require different analysis. Flip, BRRRR, rental, and wholesale strategies with key metrics and failure points.
academy.methodologyMetaDescription: How PEO produces VERIFIED ARV, confidence scoring, comp qualification, and triage gates. The analytical logic behind every output.
